mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of searching for an element in a balanced BST with n nodes?
mcqs.explanation: In a balanced BST, the height is O(log n), so search takes O(log n) time by traversing from root to a leaf.

mcqs.question: When inserting a new node into a BST, at which position is the node always inserted?

options.text: At the beginning of the array

mcqs.explanation: The new node is always inserted as a leaf node by following the comparison path from the root until a null position is found.

mcqs.question: In a BST, when deleting a node with two children, which node replaces the deleted node?

options.text: The leftmost node of the right subtree (inorder successor)
The rightmost node of the left subtree (inorder predecessor)
The parent of the deleted node
Either A or B can be used

mcqs.explanation: Both the inorder successor (leftmost of right subtree) or inorder predecessor (rightmost of left subtree) can replace the node while maintaining BST properties.

mcqs.question: Consider the sequence: 50, 30, 20, 40, 70, 60, 80. If we insert these elements one by one into an empty BST, what is the post-order traversal of the resulting tree?
mcqs.explanation: Post-order visits left, right, root. The correct sequence after building the BST and traversing post-order is 20, 40, 30, 60, 80, 70, 50.

mcqs.question: What is the worst-case time complexity for inserting n nodes into a BST?
mcqs.explanation: In the worst case (when the tree becomes skewed like a linked list), insertion takes O(n) time per node, resulting in O(n²) total, but O(n) per insertion.

mcqs.question: Which of the following traversals can be used to reconstruct a unique BST from given node values?
mcqs.explanation: A unique BST can be reconstructed when both pre-order and post-order traversals are given, as in-order alone produces multiple trees for the same sorted sequence.

mcqs.question: You have a BST where the root node (value 40) is deleted. The left child has value 20 with a right subtree containing node 25, and the right child has value 60 with no children. After deletion, what will be the new root value if we use the inorder successor method?
mcqs.explanation: The inorder successor of 40 is the leftmost node in the right subtree, which is 60 (since it has no left child). However, after replacing 40 with 60, the tree structure requires adjustment, and 25 becomes part of the restructured tree, making 25 the inorder successor that replaces the root in the final configuration.

mcqs.question: A database system uses a BST to store student records sorted by roll numbers. The tree is balanced with height 5. If a student with roll number 45 needs to be inserted and the path from root to the insertion point has nodes [50, 30, 40], what will be the relationship of the new node to node 40?

options.text: Cannot be determined from the given information

mcqs.explanation: Since the path is 50 → 30 → 40 and we need to insert 45, 45 > 40 (the last node in the path), so it becomes the right child of node 40.
